title: Lyon drug traffickers hire Colombian hitmen to kill their rivals
subhead: Criminal cases involving Colombian nationals have become increasingly common in the Lyon area. According to the police, these individuals are believed to be mercenaries hired by French drug trafficking kingpins to terrorize or eliminate their rivals.

title: 2030 Alps Games already facing storm of internal controversies and public backlash

China's retired rural workers are finding rest impossible
subhead: In rural China, meager pensions are forcing many retired farmers into urban exile, where survival depends on taking the most precarious jobs.

title: Dozens of troops dead in Mexican violence after drug kingpin killed
subhead: Violent clashes erupted after the death of Nemesio 'El Mencho' Oseguera, leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), was announced on Sunday, following a shootout with soldiers.

title: We fact-checked 'Marty Supreme,' the biopic of a not-so-visionary table tennis player
subhead: The biopic produced by Timothée Chalamet draws inspiration from the American champion Marty Reisman. But it was his Japanese rival, Hiroji Satoh, who brought the sport into the modern era.

title: With expected US retreat, Germany seeks to strengthen its role within NATO
subhead: Berlin has invested hundreds of billions of euros to rearm since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022 and aims to play a major role within the North Atlantic Alliance, particularly as the US moves toward isolation.

2026 Winter Olympics: New infrastructure raises financial and environmental questions
subhead: The anticipated increase in visitor numbers to the protected Alpine regions is the first major impact expected from the Games. Critics have condemned what they call the 'gentrification of the mountains.'

title: Oleksandr Syrsky, commander of the Ukrainian army, to Le Monde: 'Russian army losses have exceeded its recruitment level'
subhead: The Ukrainian commander-in-chief told Le Monde that, despite a 'very difficult' situation on the battlefield, his army continued to resist 'the most powerful army in Europe' and to 'foil' Russian military plans.

title: In Lyon, the far right pays tribute to activist and tries to avoid controversy
subhead: Around 3,200 people marched peacefully on Saturday afternoon to condemn 'the far left,' one week after Quentin Deranque's death. The local authorities reported Nazi salutes and racist and homophobic slurs to the courts.
